covered_sentences: "How very black and angry you look," she said, "and funny and grim!"
"That's because I'm used to looking at Edgar and Isabella Linton."
Shame and pride had made him gloomy and unresponsive.
storyboard_assets.prompt:
Composition and camera: Dialogue split panel, left-right, 2:1 ratio with left as main panel. Left panel: camera positioned to the right of the fireplace at mid-room distance, angled 30 degrees inward at mid-height, looking toward both characters together; Cathy in the foreground slightly closer to the camera, Heathcliff one step behind her; front foreground is the stone fireplace edge; background shows the right stone wall only. Right panel: camera positioned behind Cathy's right shoulder, over-shoulder close shot looking toward Heathcliff's face; Cathy's right shoulder and hair edge occupy the right front foreground approximately one-fifth of the frame; background shows only a section of stone wall. Per-panel character budget: left panel 2 (Cathy + Heathcliff), right panel 1 (Heathcliff; Cathy's shoulder not counted as recognizable).

Actions and relationships: Left panel: Cathy faces Heathcliff, head tilted slightly, lips parted in an amused and questioning expression; above her head a speech bubble contains a sketch of the Linton siblings' mini-icons (light-haired boy + light-haired girl) beside a comparison arrow, labeled "used to"; Heathcliff stands with arms at his sides, gaze directed downward and to the side, lips pressed shut; above his head a speech bubble contains a question mark sketch of Cathy's mini-icon with the word "forgotten me?" — the bubble tail points toward Cathy indicating she is asking him. Right panel: Heathcliff's face in close-up, brows deeply furrowed, mouth tightly closed, eyes cast down — expressing shame and proud withdrawal; no speech bubble.

Scene: Wuthering Heights ground-floor main sitting room, daytime, late December; fireplace area is the activity center with iron grate visible; Cathy stands in front of the fireplace stone step, facing Heathcliff; Heathcliff stands approximately half a step directly in front of her, beside a dark wood hard chair, chair back at approximately his waist height.

covered_sentences: "Go on, Heathcliff," said Hindley, condescendingly, "you are allowed to shake her hand."
"I will not!" cried Heathcliff at last.

covered_sentences: "I did not mean to laugh at you, Heathcliff," said Cathy.
"It was only that you looked strange. If you wash your face and brush your hair, you will be all right, but you are so dirty."
"I like to be dirty!" cried Heathcliff, and he left the room.

covered_sentences: Hindley and Frances were very amused by this scene, but Cathy was deeply disturbed by it.
She did not know what she had said to upset him so.
Hindley and Frances took Cathy off to the drawing room to show her the presents they had bought for the young Lintons.
Edgar and Isabella had been invited to Wuthering Heights the following morning, and their mother had agreed that they could come on condition they were kept away from that gypsy boy.
